# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment that pulls our application image from Docker Hub. Use the latest tag so we always get the newest version automatically. This is for our development environment where we want continuous updates without manual intervention.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-deployment
  namespace: development
  labels:
    app: my-application
    environment: development
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-application
      environment: development
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-application
        environment: development
    spec:
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        runAsGroup: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
        seccompProfile:
          type: RuntimeDefault
      containers:
        - name: my-application
          # IMPORTANT: Using :latest tag is generally discouraged even in dev environments.
          # It makes rollbacks difficult, breaks reproducibility, and you lose track of
          # what's actually deployed. Prefer pinning to a specific image digest or semantic
          # version tag (e.g., my-org/my-application:1.2.3 or my-org/my-application@sha256:abcdef...).
          # If you must use latest for rapid dev iteration, combine with imagePullPolicy: Always
          # and understand the trade-offs.
          image: my-org/my-application:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
              protocol: TCP
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            runAsNonRoot: true
            runAsUser: 1000
            runAsGroup: 1000
            capabilities:
              drop:
                - ALL
            seccompProfile:
              type: RuntimeDefault
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "128Mi"
              cpu: "100m"
            limits:
              memory: "256Mi"
              cpu: "500m"
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 20
            timeoutSeconds: 5
            failureThreshold: 3
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ready
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 3
            failureThreshold: 3
          volumeMounts:
            - name: tmp-volume
              mountPath: /tmp
      volumes:
        - name: tmp-volume
          emptyDir:
            sizeLimit: "100Mi"
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: dockerhub-credentials
